outcome_contract.why: Free cells between close bookings look identical to genuinely free nights, hiding scheduling inefficiencies that staff need to act on.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: Short gaps (1ظô3 consecutive free days between bookings) are visually distinguished on the grid, giving staff an immediate visual cue to investigate or reassign.

engineering_coverage.applicable: Gap period flows through existing `theme["date.status"]` ظْ `topColor` ظْ `Day` component; amber colour defined once in `statusColors`
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Colour override in `RowCell` directly; `StatusLegend` still manually defined
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Option A: single colour definition in `statusColors`; cleaner legend sync

engineering_coverage.applicable: `getDayParams` special-case ensures full-block rendering; adjacent bookings unaffected
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Same result achievable but requires more component logic
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Option A: cleaner; `single.full` matches visual intent

engineering_coverage.applicable: Gap detection is a pure function in data hook ظ¤ easily unit tested; 5 TCs cover threshold, boundaries
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Harder to test: requires rendering component with gap date set; more setup

engineering_coverage.applicable: `MyLocalStatus` extended; `statusColors` extended; `TBookingPeriod` satisfied with placeholders; `NON_OCCUPIED_STATUSES` updated; `getDayParams` one-line addition
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: `TRow`, `TReservedPeriod`, `RowCellProps` all need new fields; larger contract surface

engineering_coverage.applicable: O(n├ùd) post-processing pass inside `useMemo`; trivial for typical grid windows
Additive; revert = remove `"gap"` from union + colour + detection + `getDayParams` line

stage_payload.decision_summary: Decide where to detect and represent 1ظô3 day free gaps between bookings so they render in a distinct amber colour on the rooms grid. Two viable approaches: inject the gap status into the data layer (`useGridData`) vs. compute and apply it in the component layer (`RowCell`/`RoomGrid`). Additionally, because `getDayParams` controls day-shape rendering, any injected gap period must be handled specially in `getDayParams` to avoid shape distortion on adjacent cells.
goals: Gap cells render amber/warning on the grid without touching the ReservationGrid rendering engine
No false positives for arrival/departure counts or occupancy strip
Clean TypeScript: exhaustive `statusColors` record, no `as any`
Minimal file surface ظ¤ additive change, rollback = revert commit
assumptions: Gap detection operates on fully packed rows (after `packBookingsIntoRows`)

evaluation_criteria.Why it matters: Gap cells must show as full-block amber fill, not as booking start/end shapes
Must not corrupt occupancy, arrival, or departure counts
TypeScript must compile without casts or suppressions
Gap detection must be unit-testable without Firebase

options_considered.Description: Add `"gap"` to `MyLocalStatus`. In `useGridData`, after `packBookingsIntoRows`, inject synthetic `TBookingPeriod` entries for gap dates. Add `status === "gap"` special-case in `getDayParams` (like `"disabled"`). Add `"gap"` to `NON_OCCUPIED_STATUSES`. Guard arrival/departure loops.
options_considered.Upside: Single source of truth; zero rendering code changes; gap status flows through existing `theme["date.status"]` colour lookup; pure-function detection is easily tested
options_considered.Downside: Requires touching `dateUtils.ts` (`getDayParams`) to add one special-case; TBookingPeriod has required fields needing placeholder values for synthetic records
options_considered.Key risks: Forgetting `NON_OCCUPIED_STATUSES` update inflates occupancy; forgetting `getDayParams` special-case causes shape distortion

options_considered.Description: Pass a `gapDates: Set<string>` or similar from `useGridData` to `RoomGrid`/`RowCell`. In `RowCell`, override colour when date is in `gapDates`. No change to `getDayParams`.
options_considered.Upside: Avoids `getDayParams` change; rendering logic stays in component
options_considered.Downside: Prop-drilling through `GridReservationRow` ظْ `ReservationGrid` ظْ `Row` ظْ `RowCell`; bypasses the `theme["date.status"]` colour mechanism; requires interface changes to `TRow`, `TReservedPeriod`, `RowCellProps`; much larger surface area
options_considered.Key risks: Interface sprawl across 4ظô5 files; `StatusLegend` colour still manually defined; harder to test rendering path

chosen_approach.Recommendation: Option A ظ¤ data-layer injection with `getDayParams` special-case
chosen_approach.Why this wins: Minimal surface area; gap status flows through existing colour mechanism; zero changes to `ReservationGrid`, `Row`, or `RowCell` prop interfaces; pure-function detection is easily unit tested; `StatusLegend` entry is the only manual sync point.
chosen_approach.Option B (component-layer) ظ¤ rejected: prop-drilling through 4ظô5 interface files; bypasses existing colour mechanism; harder to test; larger blast radius.

rejected_approaches: Option B (component-layer) ظ¤ rejected: prop-drilling through 4ظô5 interface files; bypasses existing colour mechanism; harder to test; larger blast radius.

validation_implications: TypeScript compile enforces `statusColors` exhaustiveness ظ¤ partial changes cause build failure
Unit tests for gap detection algorithm are the primary validation
sequencing_constraints: `MyLocalStatus` change must be committed alongside `statusColors` change (single task, single commit)

process_topology.note: None: no material process topology change
